stem: A factory produces glass bottles using two sources. Furnace Alpha handles 52% of production with a known defect rate of 1.9%. Furnace Beta handles 48% of production, but its defect rate is uncertain — recent quality audits suggest it lies between 5.5% and 8.1%, depending on raw material batch quality. A randomly selected glass bottle is found to be defective. What is the probability it came from Furnace Beta?

ground_truth.answer: P(Furnace Beta | defective) ranges from 0.728 to 0.797 as Furnace Beta's defect rate varies from 5.5% to 8.1%. Point estimate at 6.8%: 0.768.

solution_steps: Apply Bayes' theorem: P(B|def) = P(def|B)×P(B) / [P(def|A)×P(A) + P(def|B)×P(B)].
The interval [0.728, 0.797] captures the range of posterior probabilities.
common_failure_modes: Using only the midpoint defect rate and providing a single answer
Comparing defect rates directly without applying Bayes' theorem
